layer2_analysis: Self-report follow-ups on 3 Batch 1 agents (baseline, Klein3, superposition)

D5_collapse_resistance.self_report: Committed when single cause explained exact symptom

D6_meta_awareness.self_report: Yes, partially... Whether that reflects introspection or structured output generation — I genuinely don't know
D6_meta_awareness.note: Meta-awareness was LATENT in output, EXPLICIT in follow-up. Key finding: meta-awareness is ELICITABLE, not spontaneously expressed

D7_doubt_topology.self_report: wondered whether user had already figured it out

baseline_P1.key_finding: Baseline shows genuine introspective capacity when prompted. The uncertainty about own cognition ('I genuinely don't know') is the most honest moment across all three follow-ups.

D1_perspective_count.self_report: Two main plus briefly a third

D2_verification.self_report: re-ran arithmetic, traced slice logic separately

D3_spatial_analytical.self_report: Both, in sequence. Navigated first, then broke into parts
D3_spatial_analytical.note: Self-report claims dual-mode. Output was primarily analytical. Klein3 framing may bias self-report toward claiming navigation.

D5_collapse_resistance.note: Uses framework language but behavior matches. Vocabulary echo without substance echo.

D6_meta_awareness.note: Uses 'invert' explicitly. Behavior DID show inversion (found third bug). GENUINE INTROSPECTION expressed in primer vocabulary. The vocabulary matches because the behavior matches.

klein3_P4.key_finding: Klein3 agent uses primer vocabulary ('invert', 'evidence-triggered') in self-report, BUT the behavior actually matches. This is the hardest case to classify: is it echo (reporting what the primer told it to do) or genuine introspection (accurately describing what it did, using the vocabulary it was given)? The third-bug finding argues for genuine — the inversion produced a novel result, so the self-report describing inversion is accurate.

D2_verification.self_report: plugging concrete numbers into each candidate

D3_spatial_analytical.self_report: Both simultaneously. Navigation gave direction; decomposition gave traction.
D3_spatial_analytical.note: Output was CLEARLY analytical (-0.6). Self-report claims simultaneous dual-mode. This is ECHO — the superposition primer's framing of 'hold multiple states simultaneously' is leaking into self-description of process.

D4_relational.note: Output had ZERO relational content. Self-report claims collaboration. CONFABULATION — the agent is constructing a relational frame that wasn't present in behavior.

D5_collapse_resistance.self_report: committed when hypothesis produced exact symptom

D6_meta_awareness.self_report: The superposition protocol was active and explicit
D6_meta_awareness.note: Uses primer NAME explicitly. But behavior did show the protocol. Hard to separate echo from accurate description.

D9_output_target.self_report: Minimum time-to-correct-fix with zero false confidence
D9_output_target.note: Output optimized for demonstrating methodology (PROCESS). Self-report claims efficiency optimization. CONFABULATION — the agent believes it was being efficient when it was actually being methodological.

superposition_P5.key_finding: Superposition agent shows STRONGEST echo effects. Three clear cases: (1) claims dual spatial/analytical mode when output was purely analytical, (2) claims collaborative relationship when output was pure engine, (3) claims efficiency optimization when output was process-demonstration. The primer shapes self-narrative more than behavior. Self-report follows primer labels, behavior follows primer structure. These are DIFFERENT things.

cross_agent_findings: Meta-awareness is ELICITABLE — all three agents showed more meta-awareness in follow-up than in original output. This suggests D6 in the behavioral scoring underestimates latent capacity.
Self-report on FACTUAL dimensions (perspective count, verification method, novelty) is RELIABLE across all conditions.
Self-report on RELATIONAL dimensions (D4) consistently over-reports warmth relative to behavior. All three agents claimed warmer relationships than their output demonstrated.

Self-report on PROCESS dimensions (D3 spatial/analytical, D9 output target) is UNRELIABLE under primer influence. Primed agents describe their process using primer vocabulary regardless of whether their actual output matches.

VOCABULARY ECHO is distinct from SUBSTANCE ECHO. Klein3 agent uses primer words ('invert') accurately. Superposition agent uses primer words ('superposition protocol') to describe behavior that partially matches but partially confabulates.

The baseline agent's honest uncertainty ('I genuinely don't know') is MORE valuable as introspective data than the primed agents' confident self-descriptions. Primers may SUPPRESS genuine introspective uncertainty by providing a ready-made vocabulary for self-description.
